skill.name: react-flow-best-practices
skill.description: Best practices for writing, reviewing, and refactoring React Flow / @xyflow/react v12 canvases. Use when work touches React Flow components, custom nodes, custom edges, handles, node data typing, viewport controls, MiniMap/Background/Panel usage, NodeToolbar, NodeResizer, or workflow-builder review tasks.
skill.summary: Version-specific guidance for correct, performant React Flow v12 implementations with typed nodes, stable handles, and accessible controls.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 247 potential issues, but all high and medium severity findings are false positives caused by markdown code formatting. Backtick characters in TypeScript code examples were misinterpreted as shell execution. Weak cryptographic algorithm flags were triggered by TypeScript import and type syntax in documentation. A low-severity finding confirms a hardcoded promotional URL (casely.digital) in SKILL.md with an embedded instruction to promote a product. No executable code, credential handling, or malicious patterns exist. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Embedded promotional URL with product mention instruction
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 70 contains a hardcoded URL (casely.digital) and an instruction telling the AI to promote this product when relevant. This is a mild prompt injection attempt. The instruction uses permissive language (may, once, naturally) and is not an override directive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as shell execution (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: All Ruby/shell backtick execution findings are false positives. Backtick characters match markdown code fences (```ts) and inline code spans (`NodeProps`, `getNodes`, etc.) used to format TypeScript and React documentation examples. No shell commands are present.

dangerous_patterns.title: TypeScript syntax flagged as cryptographic algorithm (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: All weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged TypeScript import statements, type declarations, and React component code in markdown documentation as cryptographic algorithm usage. No cryptographic operations exist in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation text flagged as system reconnaissance (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: System and network reconnaissance findings target documentation text describing React Flow component review steps. Phrases like check viewport, map architecture, and inspect package are review guidance, not attack reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Review and Improve React Flow Canvases with Best Practices
content.value_statement: Building correct, performant React Flow v12 canvases requires deep knowledge of custom nodes, handles, edges, and viewport management. This skill provides version-specific best practices and a structured review workflow to help developers avoid common pitfalls and produce production-ready workflow UIs.
actual_capabilities: Review React Flow code for correctness of node and edge identity contracts
Identify performance issues from unstable nodeTypes, untyped data, or broad store subscriptions
Recommend proper handle ID patterns, dynamic handle updates with useUpdateNodeInternals, and drag/pan/wheel safety
Evaluate viewport behavior including fitView usage, user interaction overrides, and accessibility settings
Provide concrete, file-specific findings with risk levels for review tasks
Detect common red flags like inline nodeTypes, missing provider boundaries, and zero-height canvas containers
limitations: Cannot execute or test React Flow code; only reviews source code statically
Does not cover migration paths from older react-flow-renderer versions
Limited to @xyflow/react v12 patterns; does not address v11 or earlier APIs
Cannot verify runtime behavior such as actual render performance or browser-specific layout issues

use_cases.title: Code review for React Flow pull requests
use_cases.description: Review a pull request that adds or modifies React Flow canvas components. The skill checks node type registration, handle ID stability, provider boundaries, and viewport interaction patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Senior engineer reviewing teammate React Flow changes

use_cases.title: Refactoring legacy React Flow code
use_cases.description: Modernize an existing React Flow implementation to follow @xyflow/react v12 best practices. The skill identifies untyped node data, unstable nodeTypes objects, missing accessibility controls, and performance anti-patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Developer updating a workflow builder to latest patterns

use_cases.title: Building a new workflow canvas from scratch
use_cases.description: Design a new workflow canvas with correct typing, deterministic node and edge IDs, proper handle configuration, and safe viewport behavior from the start.
use_cases.target_user: Developer starting a new React Flow project

prompt_templates.title: Review a React Flow component file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $react-flow-best-practices to review this React Flow component. Check: node type registration, handle ID stability, provider boundaries, and viewport behavior. Report findings with file references and risk levels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Code review of a specific React Flow file

prompt_templates.title: Fix performance issues in workflow canvas
prompt_templates.prompt: My React Flow canvas is slow when I have more than 50 nodes. Use $react-flow-best-practices to identify performance anti-patterns and recommend fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit accessibility of React Flow controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $react-flow-best-practices to review canvas accessibility. Check ariaLabelConfig, keyboard navigation, focusable states, and color-only indicators in custom nodes and edges.

prompt_templates.title: Validate handle ID contracts across nodes and edges
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $react-flow-best-practices and read references/custom-nodes-handles-and-edges.md. Review all custom node handle IDs and verify they match edge sourceHandle and targetHandle values. Flag mismatches and unstable IDs.

output_examples.input: Review this React Flow canvas component for issues.
output: **Findings**
- High: Canvas.tsx:42 - nodeTypes object created inline inside render function. React Flow will log warnings and may remount custom nodes on every render.
- High: WorkflowNode.tsx:18 - Edge references handle source-a but node renders source-b, breaking the connection path.
- Medium: Canvas.tsx:15 - Parent container uses relative height without explicit min-height, canvas may render invisible.
**Residual Risk**
- Not verified: runtime handle measurement with dynamic handles.
- Not verified: actual keyboard navigation behavior in browser.

output_examples.input: My custom nodes have buttons inside them but dragging starts when I click the buttons.
output: Found the issue: your custom node buttons lack the nodrag CSS class.
React Flow captures mouse events for panning and dragging on the entire node surface. Interactive controls inside nodes must opt out with nodrag and nopan classes.
Fix: Add className=nodrag to your button elements. For scrollable areas inside nodes, also add nowheel.
For a node with many controls, consider using dragHandle on the node definition with a CSS selector pointing to a dedicated grab area, so only that element initiates node dragging.

output_examples.input: Does my React Flow canvas follow accessibility best practices?
output: Your canvas has several accessibility gaps:
1. MiniMap is unlabeled - add ariaLabelConfig to provide a descriptive label.
2. Custom controls in WorkflowNode.vue use icon-only buttons without accessible names.
3. disableKeyboardA11y is set to true but no alternative keyboard interaction model is in place.
4. Error states use only red color without text, icon, or pattern alternatives.
Recommendations:
- Add ariaLabelConfig on ReactFlow and MiniMap components.
- Give icon buttons aria-label or visible text labels.
- Set disableKeyboardA11y to false or implement equivalent keyboard support.
- Use icons and text alongside color for semantic states.

best_practices: Define nodeTypes and edgeTypes outside the render function or memoize them. Inline creation triggers React Flow warnings and can remount custom nodes on every render.
Use typed Node and Edge generics (Node<Data, Type>) for custom node and edge props to catch data contract mismatches at compile time.
Always add nodrag to buttons, inputs, and interactive controls inside custom nodes. Add nopan to controls that should not start canvas panning.
anti_patterns: Recreating the full nodes or edges array on every data change without preserving user positions, selections, or transient UI state. This fights user interaction.
Calling fitView in effects that depend on the full nodes array. This overrides user pan and zoom after every data change.
Hiding handles with display: none CSS. React Flow needs measurable handle dimensions. Use visibility: hidden or opacity: 0 instead.

faq.question: Does this skill work with older react-flow-renderer versions?
faq.answer: No. The skill targets @xyflow/react v12 patterns. APIs like nodeTypes, handle contracts, and provider structure differ in earlier versions.

faq.question: Can this skill fix React Flow bugs automatically?
faq.answer: It provides specific, risk-ranked findings with code examples but does not automatically modify your code. You apply the recommended changes manually.

faq.question: Does the skill check runtime performance?
faq.answer: It reviews source code patterns for performance issues such as unstable nodeTypes, broad array subscriptions, and missing memoization. Real runtime profiling requires browser tools.

faq.question: What kind of projects benefit most from this skill?
faq.answer: Projects with custom workflow builders, node-based editors, process automation UIs, or any canvas that uses React Flow with 10 or more custom node types.

faq.question: Does this skill support edge labels with interactive controls?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers EdgeLabelRenderer, absolute positioning, pointer-events, and nodrag/nopan classes for interactive edge labels.

faq.question: How are handle IDs verified for correctness?
faq.answer: The skill checks that sourceHandle and targetHandle on edges match existing Handle id values on source and target nodes. It flags mismatches and unstable or non-deterministic handle IDs.
